title: Introduction to Problem Solving with Python
description: A structured tutorial sequence on Python fundamentals, control flow, functions, loops, and list-based problem solving.
syllabus: This tutorial stream is practice-first: each topic starts with a short concept pass, then coding drills, then problem solving. Focus is on clean logic, readable code, and debugging through code-tracing.
outcome: Students can solve beginner Python problems independently using conditionals, loops, functions, and list-processing patterns.

schedule.topic: Getting started: variables, basic I/O, and expressions
Numbers, variables, typed input, and arithmetic workflows
Boolean logic, operators, and nested conditional decision-making
Function decomposition, loops, and core numeric problem patterns
Lists, built-in list methods, and custom list-processing functions

notebooks.title: Getting started: variables, basic I/O, and expressions
Print formatting in depth: sep, end, newline, and output control
Numbers and understanding data through structured output
Variable input, type-casting, and arithmetic workflows
Boolean logic and arithmetic operators with truth-table reasoning
Basic function decomposition and extended conditional problem-solving
Loops: numeric series, factorial, prime tests, and Fibonacci
Lists with loops and core list built-in functions
Custom list-processing functions and integrated practice

title: Introduction to Programming with Python
description: Step-by-step Python tutorials for beginners, focused on computational thinking and practical coding.
syllabus: A gentle introduction to Python covering core programming concepts. Students learn to think computationally and solve real-world problems step by step.
outcome: Students can write basic Python programs, use loops and conditionals, define functions, and work with lists and strings.

schedule.topic: Python setup, variables, data types, and basic I/O
Loops: for, while, and nested loops
Lists, strings, and basic data manipulation

title: Data Structures
description: Tutorial sessions on foundational data structures — arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs — with problem solving in Python.
syllabus: Tutorial sessions reinforce the lecture concepts through implementation exercises. Each session implements a data structure from scratch and benchmarks it against Python built-ins.
outcome: Students can implement and use core data structures, analyze time/space complexity, and apply them to algorithmic problem solving.

schedule.topic: Linked lists: singly and doubly linked

notebooks.title: Stack and queue using Python lists

resources.title: Problem Solving with Algorithms and Data Structures

title: Fundamentals of Computer Systems
description: Tutorial sessions covering computer-system concepts and practical office-suite skills for academic and professional use.
syllabus: Covers the basics of computer hardware, software, operating systems, and essential Microsoft Office skills (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) needed for academic work.
outcome: Students understand fundamental computing concepts and can use office productivity tools effectively for academic reporting and presentations.

schedule.topic: Microsoft Word: formatting and document design
Microsoft Excel: formulas, charts, and data analysis

title: Numerical Methods Lab
description: Lab worksheets and assignments for numerical methods — root-finding, interpolation, numerical integration, and ODEs — implemented in Python.
syllabus: Lab sessions implement numerical algorithms from scratch using Python and NumPy, then validate results against SciPy. Focus is on understanding the algorithm, not just using a library.
outcome: Students can implement and analyze core numerical methods, understand their error characteristics, and apply them to scientific computing problems.

schedule.topic: Floating point arithmetic and error analysis
Linear systems: Gaussian elimination and LU factorization
Ordinary differential equations: Euler and Runge-Kutta

notebooks.title: Numerical integration: trapezoidal and Simpson's rule

resources.title: Numerical Methods for Engineers — Chapra & Canale
